personal.name: Mahammad Siraj Cheruvu
personal.title: Software Engineer · AI Engineer · Data Engineer
personal.tagline: I build production software, design intelligent AI systems, and engineer scalable data pipelines — end to end.

about: I'm a graduate student in AI and Business Analytics at USF (GPA 3.88) who operates across three domains: I engineer reliable software — REST APIs, clean backend architecture, and full-stack features; I build AI systems — multi-agent LLM pipelines, RAG frameworks, and production-grade ML workflows; and I design data infrastructure — real-time Kafka/Spark ETL pipelines, analytics dashboards, and scalable storage solutions. My goal is always the same: turn complex problems into working, production-quality systems.

education.degree: Masters in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics
B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering

bullets: Collected, cleaned, and maintained departmental data to build Tableau and Power BI analytics dashboards, enabling data-driven reporting for university staff.
Supported student onboarding and front-desk operations in a fast-paced university environment.
Led backend development using CLEAN architecture, building RESTful APIs with ASP.NET — reducing integration time by 30%.
Designed and deployed an MS SQL database managing 1000+ vendor and expense records with secure access control.
Developed front-end features with JavaScript ES6 and integrated vendor management, invoice generation, and document signing — improving process efficiency by 25%.
Enabled secure file storage in Azure Blob Storage and integrated PDF generation and automated email features, cutting document turnaround time by 40%.

projects.title: IntelliMove — Talent-to-Role Matching
projects.description: AI-powered system that evaluates employee skill profiles against job descriptions using multi-factor scoring — accounting for skill decay, confidence levels, partial category credit, and sentiment analysis from performance reviews.
bullets: LLM-powered skill normalization via Claude (AWS Bedrock) with hierarchical skill grouping and a decay function that penalizes stale skills over time.
Confidence scoring aggregated from resumes, manager reviews, and peer feedback, with sentiment modifiers adjusting final match scores.
Achieved 96.2% fit accuracy on test dataset (9 employees × 5 roles), successfully differentiating managerial vs. IC role requirements.

projects.title: TenantLens — Rental Transparency Platform
projects.description: Renter-focused web application that combats rental fraud through community-driven property reviews, scam reporting, and document-verified residency badges.
bullets: Built with .NET 10 (C#) and Azure — uses Azure Key Vault and Managed Identity for secure credential management in production.
Document-based residency verification with configurable address-matching thresholds to issue trusted 'verified stay' badges.
Addresses a real gap in the rental market: trustworthy, renter-reported property intelligence to counter fraudulent listings.

projects.title: Bus Headway — Real-Time Stream Processing
projects.description: Distributed streaming pipeline that monitors transit service intervals in real time, detecting bus bunching and service gaps to trigger live operational alerts.
bullets: Kafka–Spark Structured Streaming pipeline with stateful event-time aggregations using Kappa Architecture for continuous stream processing.
Containerized multi-worker deployment via Docker Compose — Spark, Kafka, and MongoDB running as fault-tolerant, low-latency services.
Hourly performance metrics aggregation with anomaly detection that flags disruptions before they compound into larger delays.

projects.title: ViGen — Story to Scene Video Pipeline
projects.description: Transforms a user-guided concept (tone, genre, topic, scene count) into a complete narrated video — story generation, scene planning, video synthesis, and audio all automated end-to-end.
bullets: LangChain multi-agent pipeline: StoryAgent generates structured scene scripts, SceneAgent plans visuals, with async video and audio generation running in parallel.
Amazon Nova Reel (AWS Bedrock) for AI video synthesis + Amazon Polly for narration — assembled into final MP4 via FFmpeg with per-scene muxing.
Includes automatic prompt softening logic to handle content filter edge cases, ensuring reliable generation across diverse story themes.

projects.title: LLM-Agent — Modular AI Agent Framework
projects.description: LLM-agnostic agent framework that lets you build reasoning agents with tools, memory, and RAG — swap between Gemini, Ollama, or Groq with no code changes.
bullets: Multi-LLM support (Google Gemini, Ollama, Groq) via a plug-and-play provider abstraction — cloud or local deployment, same interface.
Lightweight RAG using FAISS + SentenceTransformers for vector retrieval, with built-in tools: Python execution, Serper web search, web scraping, and file I/O.
Modular architecture with configurable agent roles, goals, and backstories — deploy as a FastAPI service with SQLite persistence, hosted on Render.

projects.title: Stock Vision — ML Stock Forecasting Platform
projects.description: Full-stack web app that forecasts stock prices using three ML models and Twitter sentiment analysis, delivering actionable BUY/SELL signals on a live dashboard of 20 major stocks.
bullets: Three forecasting models per stock: LSTM neural networks, Linear Regression with trend analysis, and ARIMA time-series — signals synthesized into consensus recommendations.
Sentiment pipeline scrapes Twitter with snscrape, scores tweets via TextBlob and spaCy, and weights sentiment as a modifier on model output.
React 18 frontend (Tailwind CSS, Highcharts, Plotly, D3.js) over a Flask backend — tracks TSLA, NVDA, AAPL, GOOG, AMZN and 15 more with 7-day forecasts.
